mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 200 index points, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 201.7 both satisfy trigger conditions
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 136,018 thousand persons provides baseline for trend detection
failure_condition: Monthly-over-month PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 58% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 368,861 million dollars and HOUST at 1,486 thousand units provide baseline for growth calculation
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both growth conditions are simultaneously met, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.3% month-over-month indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profitability and equity market performance, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 112.3227 (2017=100) provides baseline for momentum calculation
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 54% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.7%, it signals labor market softening that reduces consumer confidence and spending, triggering negative equity returns as investors anticipate earnings pressure and potential recession, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.5% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: UNRATE rises above 4.7% but SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of subsequent instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration that constrains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11,450.5 billion chained 2017 dollars provides baseline for growth calculation
failure_condition: Subtrend GDP growth does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 56% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) from current elevated levels signals monetary policy easing that reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing equity discount rates and supporting valuation expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% is at elevated levels where rate cuts would signal policy shift
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle transitioning to easing
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25% but SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of subsequent instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month by more than 3.0% while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract, it signals a synchronized demand shock across residential and consumer spending that depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 3.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,486 thousand units and RSAFS at 368,861 million dollars provide baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: Both demand indicators decline simultaneously but SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
